title: Guru Granth Sahib
subtitle: The Holy Scripture of Sikhism
description: The central religious scripture of Sikhism, regarded by Sikhs as the final, sovereign, and living Guru. It was compiled by Guru Arjan and later expanded by Guru Gobind Singh.
summary: The Guru Granth Sahib contains hymns and verses composed by six Sikh Gurus and various Hindu and Muslim saints. It emphasizes monotheism, equality, selfless service, meditation on God's name, and the rejection of caste and ritualism.
originalLanguage: Punjabi, Sanskrit, Hindi, Persian, Braj Bhasha

chapters.title: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: Ik Onkar – There is One God. Truth is His Name. He is the Creator. He is without fear. He is without hatred. Timeless is His form. Beyond birth. Self-existent. By Guru's grace.
He is beyond time. He cannot be described. By hearing, seeing, and speaking, He is purified.
By thinking, He cannot be reduced to thought, even by thinking hundreds of thousands of times. By remaining silent, inner silence is not obtained, even by remaining lovingly absorbed deep within.
The hunger of the hungry is appeased by eating; even by eating hundreds of thousands of tons of food, hunger is not appeased.
Through the Guru's Teachings, the Great One is known. He cannot be known by boasting.
There is no shortage of those who speak; who speaks the truth? Only that person is honored, O Lord, who does what You say.
In the city of the body, there are many lamps. By the Guru's Light, the Divine Light is seen.

chapters.title: Rahi Ras — The Evening Prayer (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: O Lord, we are Your sacrifice. We are Your servants. We worship You and meditate on Your name.
The night is passing away and the morning dawn approaches. Let us sing the songs of the Lord's praises.
The world is a dream. In a moment, it is gone. Only the Name of the Lord endures.
The Lord's name is the cure for all sorrow. The Lord's name is the support of the soul.
Night and day, sing the Lord's praises. Through the Guru's Teachings, the Lord is found.
The Lord's light pervades all places. Through the Guru, the light is seen.

chapters.title: Anand Sahib — The Song of Bliss (Guru Amar Das)

verses.text: By great good fortune, the Lord is met. By great good fortune, the Lord is found.
Bliss is in the Lord's name. Bliss is in the Lord's service.
Through the Guru, the Lord is known. Through the Guru, the Lord is found.

chapters.title: Sukhmani Sahib — Psalm of Peace (Guru Arjan, Ashtpadi 1)

verses.text: Blessed, blessed is the Guru who reveals the Name of the Lord.
Those who are in love with the Lord, what can death do to them?
The Lord is the inner friend of all. He accompanies all through life and death.

chapters.title: Sukhmani Sahib — Psalm of Peace (Guru Arjan, Ashtpadi 2)

verses.text: The body is the city, the senses are the gates, and the mind is the gatekeeper. Through the Guru's teachings, the True Lord comes to dwell within.
The mind is stained by worldly attachment. Through the Guru's teachings, it is cleansed.

chapters.title: Wadhans Chhant — Hymns of the Lord (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: The Lord's name is the boat to cross the ocean of suffering. The Guru is the helmsman who steers the boat.
Those who meditate on the Lord's name are liberated. They swim across the ocean of pain.
Tell me, O Jogi, what is the highest religion? Tell me the essence of truth.
The highest religion is to erase pride and conceit. The highest religion is to serve others.
The soul-bride is colored with the Lord's love. She yearns to meet her Beloved.
Through the Guru's grace, the Beloved is found. The soul-bride is united with the Lord.
The world is transient. Why are you attached to it? The body will perish; only the Name endures.
Meditate on the Lord, O my mind. The Lord is the true support of all.

chapters.title: Anand Sahib — The Song of Bliss (Guru Amar Das)

verses.text: I am Thine, O Lord, I am Thine; all mine are Thine, O Great King. My body, my breath, my wealth and my soul are Thine, O Lord; everything is Thine.
He is the treasure of virtue, the fountain of peace. He is the ocean of mercy, the form of bliss. He is the remover of pain, the bestower of peace.
The Guru has shown me the Lord's path, and wiped away my sorrows. Nanak, the servant, sings of the Lord's praises.

chapters.title: Sukhmani Sahib — The Psalm of Peace (Guru Arjan)

verses.text: In the first quarter, meditate on the Lord, the Fearless One, who removes all fears. Remembering Him, your fears shall depart.
O mind, why do you wander lost, without your Beloved? Apply yourself to the Guru's Word, and the Lord shall come to your home.
He who has the Lord's blessing can never be harmed. His doubts are dispelled and his sorrows are removed.
By singing the Lord's praises, one obtains supreme peace. The Guru's instruction is to meditate on the Lord's name.

chapters.title: Gauri — The Bride of Light (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: O Lord, through devotion I have found the jewel of Thy name. By Thy grace, I have obtained the treasure of the Guru's wisdom.
The Lord's love is a deep ocean; how can I cross it? By the Guru's boat I cross the world ocean.
There is but one God. True is His Name, creative His personality, and immortal His form.
He is the same for all ages. He is beyond time. He is the Creator, without fear, without hate.

chapters.title: Maru — On the Nature of God (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: The Lord's name is the support of the earth. Without the Name, the world is wasted away.
The Lord's ways are beyond human understanding. Through the Guru's grace, the mystery is revealed.

chapters.title: Gaund — The Power of God (Guru Nanak)

verses.text: The Lord's power is beyond measure. He sustains the universe and dwells within every heart.
Without the Guru, no one can know the Lord. Through the Guru's Word, the path is illuminated.
O mind, do not be proud of your wealth and youth. They shall pass away like the dew on the grass.
Only the Name of the Lord is true; everything else is false. Meditate on the Lord, O my mind.
